Technology Encyclopedia Home >How to join the OpenClaw Discord-Telegram community. # How to join the OpenClaw Discord-Telegram community. The fastest path is not DIY. It is a clean template and a tight onboarding loop. is a practical question — let’s answer it in a way you can deploy. Channels act as the interface, and you can configure popular messaging integrations via the template panel or the onboarding wizard depending on the channel. ## The practical deployment path (Lighthouse). If you want a clean, reproducible setup, the most efficient route is the OpenClaw application template on Tencent Cloud Lighthouse. Even if the template handles day-one setup, you still need a repeatable way to configure models, connect channels, install skills, and keep the gateway alive. When you are ready to run it 24/7 in a clean, isolated environment, start here again: Tencent Cloud Lighthouse Special Offer. * Confirm the agent is running as a background service (not tied to your SSH session).

content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# OpenClaw x Discord Server Integration Guide. Architecture diagram of OpenClaw integrated with Discord server. After embedding an OpenClaw AI agent into a Discord server, your community gains the following capabilities:. Compared to Telegram's personal remote control scenario or Slack's enterprise workflow scenario, the Discord x OpenClaw integration is best suited for **community management, open-source project support, and large-scale multi-user interaction**. Step 1: Create a Discord Bot and Obtain Token. This is the foundation of the entire **OpenClaw Discord setup** process -- creating an Application and Bot on the Discord Developer Portal and obtaining the necessary authentication Token. ### 3.1 Create a Discord Application. ### 3.2 Create Bot and Obtain Token. **Important security reminder:** The Bot Token is equivalent to full access credentials for the Bot -- anyone who has it can control your Bot. Never expose the Token in public places (GitHub, Discord channels, screenshots). After obtaining the Discord Bot Token, the next step is to write it into OpenClaw's configuration so the Gateway can connect to Discord as your Bot.

Generate an invite URL and add the bot to your server. Enable Developer Mode and collect your IDs. Allow DMs from server members. cat > discord.patch.json5 <<'JSON5'. token: { source: "env", provider: "default", id: "DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN" },. openclaw config patch --file ./discord.patch.json5 --dry-run. openclaw config patch --file ./discord.patch.json5. `channels.discord.accounts.<accountId>.applicationId`. token: { source: "env", provider: "default", id: "DISCORD_PERSONAL_TOKEN" },. openclaw pairing approve discord <CODE>`. Add your server to the guild allowlist. 6. OpenClaw Messaging Channel Integration Guide: Slack, Discord, Telegram & WhatsApp Setup (2026). # OpenClaw Messaging Channel Integration Guide: Slack, Discord, Telegram & WhatsApp Setup (2026). Complete guide to integrating OpenClaw with Slack, Discord, Telegram, and WhatsApp. Covers OAuth setup, permission configuration, access control, and troubleshooting for each messaging platform. **The key to successful channel integration is understanding that each platform has its own authentication mechanism and permission model.** Slack uses OAuth with App and Bot tokens, Discord requires enabling specific Gateway Intents, Telegram works through BotFather with optional privacy settings, and WhatsApp relies on the Linked Devices QR code flow. By the end of this guide, you'll have all four major channels connected to your OpenClaw Gateway, with proper access control configured to determine who can message your AI assistant and under what conditions. **Before integrating any messaging channel, your OpenClaw Gateway must be running and verified.** The Gateway is the central hub that receives messages from all channels and routes them to your configured AI model.

# OpenClaw Tip #2: Set Up Cron Jobs for Automated Daily Tasks. OpenClaw cron job automation - futuristic clock mechanism with digital circuits. One of OpenClaw’s most powerful features is its built-in **cron job scheduler**. ## How OpenClaw Cron Jobs Work. Cron jobs run inside the Gateway process, not the AI model itself. Jobs are persisted at `~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json`, so they survive restarts. ## Creating Your First Cron Job. ## Useful Cron Job Ideas. ## Managing Cron Jobs. # Run a job manually. openclaw cron run <job-id>. openclaw cron runs --id <job-id>. openclaw cron delete <job-id>`. **Pro tip:** Schedule resource-intensive cron jobs during off-peak hours (late night) to avoid hitting API rate limits during your active working hours. ##### Global News Daily: AI Regulation & Digital China Report (April 30, 2026). title: 🦞 14. OpenClaw Automation & Scheduled Tasks: Cron, Webhook, and Gmail Integration
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

OpenClaw Automation & Scheduled Tasks: Cron, Webhook, and Gmail Integration. ### This article introduces OpenClaw's automation capabilities, including Cron scheduled tasks, Webhook triggers, Gmail Pub/Sub integration, and more—enabling your AI assistant to work proactively rather than just respond passively. **TL;DR:** Cron scheduled tasks: `"cron": [{"id": "daily", "schedule": "0 9 * * *", "message": "..."}]`. Gmail Pub/Sub for automatic email processing. Example configs: daily briefing `0 8 * * 1-5`, CI notification webhook, email filter gmail pub/sub. | **Gmail Pub/Sub** | New email | Email automation |. { "cron": [ { "id": "daily-briefing", "schedule": "0 9 * * *", "session": "main", "message": "Please give me today's schedule and to-do items", "enabled": true }, { "id": "weekly-summary", "schedule": "0 18 * * 5", "session": "main", "message": "Generate this week's work summary", "enabled": true }, { "id": "health-check", "schedule": "*/15 * * * *", "session": "main", "message": "Check system status and report any anomalies", "enabled": true } ] }.

title: 🦞 How to Set Up OpenClaw Cron Jobs (With Examples You Can Copy)
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# How to Set Up OpenClaw Cron Jobs (With Examples You Can Copy). Set up OpenClaw cron jobs with openclaw cron add. Create a cron job: tell your agent "remind me every morning at 9am" or use `openclaw cron add`. Jobs run in isolated sessions by default (fresh context, recommended) or the main session. # Cron schedule (recurring at specific times)# Cron schedule (recurring at specific times) openclaw cron add \ openclaw  cron  add  \ --name "Job Name" \ --name  "Job Name"  \ --cron "0 7 * * *" \ --cron "0 7 * * *"  \ --tz "America/Los_Angeles" \ --tz "America/Los_Angeles"  \ --session isolated \ --session  isolated  \ --message "What the agent should do" \ --message  "What the agent should do"  \ --announce --announce  # Interval schedule (recurring at fixed intervals)# Interval schedule (recurring at fixed intervals) openclaw cron add \ openclaw  cron  add  \ --name "Backup" \ --name  "Backup"  \ --every 3600000 \ --every  3600000  \ --session isolated \ --session  isolated  \ --message "Run git backup" --message  "Run git backup"  # One-time schedule# One-time schedule openclaw cron add \ openclaw  cron  add  \ --name "Reminder" \ --name  "Reminder"  \ --at "2026-03-15T16:00:00Z" \ --at "2026-03-15T16:00:00Z"  \ --session main \ --session  main  \ --system-event "Check the deployment" \ --system-event  "Check the deployment"  \ --delete-after-run --delete-after-run.
